mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures and accommodative policy, creating a favorable environment for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in UNRATE below 3.5% signals tightening labor markets that increase wage pressures and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where UNRATE remains below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply-side capacity that supports corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 40%+ of instances where INDPRO shows positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when HOUST growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) create higher discount rates that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 40%+ of quarters with GDP growth above 3%.
